content: This qEEG narrative report has the clinical impression section removed entirely to test section validation. The confidence level is moderate.

sections.body: Patient is a 34-year-old individual presenting for quantitative EEG assessment. No history of seizure disorder. Current medications include sertraline 50mg daily. Previous neuroimaging unremarkable. Referred by treating psychiatrist for evaluation of attention difficulties.

Recording performed using 19-channel international 10-20 system montage with linked-ear reference. Impedances maintained below 5 kOhm throughout recording. Eyes-closed resting state condition for 20 minutes. Sampling rate 256 Hz.

Absolute power analysis reveals elevated theta (4-8 Hz) activity in frontal leads exceeding 1.5 standard deviations above normative database values. Alpha (8-12 Hz) power is reduced in posterior regions. Beta and delta activity are within normal limits.

Interhemispheric coherence analysis reveals reduced alpha-band coherence between homologous frontal electrode pairs. Theta-band coherence is elevated in frontocentral regions suggesting increased slow-wave synchronization.
Graph-theoretic analysis reveals reduced global efficiency in the alpha band network. Default mode network nodes show decreased connectivity strength. Small-world network properties are preserved.

Alternative explanations include anxiety disorders which can produce similar frontal theta elevations and coherence changes. Medication effects from SSRI therapy may influence spectral power distributions.

Consider neurofeedback protocol targeting theta suppression and beta enhancement at frontal sites. Repeat qEEG assessment after intervention. Integrate with neuropsychological testing results. Confidence level is indicative.

Based on a single recording session. State-dependent factors may influence findings. The normative database may not fully account for all demographic variables. qEEG measures should not be interpreted as diagnostic in isolation.
